module.title: ABC Electronics India - SAP TM End-to-End Implementation

topic.title: Lab 10: Final End-to-End Consultant Challenge (Go-Live Day)

learningObjectives.title: Prioritize multi-incident go-live situations
learningObjectives.description: Classify incidents by business impact and assign consultant capacity effectively.

learningObjectives.title: Coordinate end-to-end production recovery
learningObjectives.description: Resolve planning, execution and settlement disruptions in a single control cycle.

learningObjectives.title: Lead management communication
learningObjectives.description: Summarize impact, remediation and risk controls for executive stakeholders.

learningObjectives.title: Define preventive operating model
learningObjectives.description: Convert incident lessons into monitoring, automation and governance improvements.

sections.title: Real-Time Lab 10: Final End-to-End Consultant Challenge

sections.content: It is Monday morning after a successful Go-Live. ABC Electronics has scheduled more than 450 deliveries across India. At 8:00 AM, multiple business teams begin reporting issues simultaneously. The Incident Manager opens a War Room and assigns you as the SAP TM Functional Consultant.

sections.title: Your Mission
sections.content: Restore business operations as quickly as possible while minimizing customer impact, financial loss and operational disruption.
You have only four consultants available. Which incidents will you address first and why?

items: Why were Freight Units not generated?
Why did the carrier reject shipments?
Can the truck accident affect other deliveries?
Is the invoice dispute preventing operations?

sections.content: At 11:00 AM the CIO asks why multiple issues occurred immediately after Go-Live and requests a corrective action plan that will prevent similar incidents in the future.

Present a five-minute summary covering business impact, root causes, corrective actions, preventive actions and lessons learned. Explain not only what happened but why it happened and how similar issues can be prevented.

sections.title: Senior Consultant Thinking
sections.content: A good consultant fixes today's issue. A great consultant ensures the issue never happens again through process improvements, automation, monitoring and documentation.

sections.title: Real SAP TM Project
sections.content: During a nationwide SAP TM rollout, one company experienced more than 40 production incidents in the first week. By introducing automated monitoring for interfaces, carrier capacity, background jobs and KPI dashboards, incident volume decreased by nearly 70% within two months.

sections.title: Accenture Final Assessment Question
sections.content: If you are the SAP TM Consultant during Go-Live and multiple production issues occur simultaneously, how would you manage the situation? A strong answer includes prioritizing incidents by business impact, coordinating with cross-functional teams, restoring operations first, performing root cause analysis, communicating with stakeholders and implementing preventive measures.

items: Successfully handled end-to-end SAP TM lifecycle.
Resolved planning, execution and settlement issues.
